title: Investigate + propose fix for curmudgeon change-detection fingerprint comparison silently skipping old-schema reviews — deep-tail self-pop starvation
summary: Operator-side drift audit on 2026-05-05 found 14 WINs at 100-838% drift on detail_verdict (max: WIN-050 838%, WIN-028 837%, WIN-009 638%, WIN-036 636%) whose last curmudgeon review is a c2 dated 2026-04-07/08/09. These items have NEVER been re-reviewed despite massive accumulated drift, while less-drifted items get cycled normally by Priority 3 (change detection). Likely root cause: April-7-era fingerprints lack tldr_evidence_length and tldr_verdict_length fields (older schema, before TLDRs were a thing). When the change-detect drift comparison hits those missing fields, it likely returns NaN or silently skips them, so the overall eligibility check returns 'no significant drift' and the item never gets popped — even though detail_verdict_length grew from ~300 to ~3000 chars. Tinker investigates the actual current behavior, confirms or refutes the diagnosis, and proposes a fix. PROPOSE ONLY.

context.trigger: Operator drift audit during 2026-05-05 cowork session. Curmudgeon today did 4 reviews (3 self-pop change-detected, 1 priority-queue routed): WIN-021 c3, WIN-017 c3+c4, WIN-045 c4. None of these are in the deep tail. The 14 deep-tail items have stayed at c2 for ~28 days while curmudgeon has done dozens of self-pop reviews on other items in that window. The deep tail is structurally unreachable.
context.diagnosis_to_verify: Operator's hypothesis: curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md Step 3 says flag if 'any text field length changed by >20%'. If the comparison code does (current_len - old_len) / old_len, and old_len is undefined for tldr_* fields on c2 reviews, the result is NaN. NaN >= 20 evaluates false. If the check is per-field-OR but one field is NaN, behavior depends on implementation — could short-circuit to false, or treat as 0%. Either way, items with growth on detail_verdict_length but missing-old tldr_* fields appear to NOT be triggering. Tinker should empirically verify this by either (a) reading the curmudgeon prompt carefully and tracing the logic, or (b) running a synthetic compare on real WIN-050 fingerprints to see what curmudgeon would output.

audit_data.drift_examples: WIN-050 detail_verdict_length grew 838% since c2; WIN-028 grew 837%; WIN-009 grew 638%; all from c2 reviews dated 2026-04-07. None re-reviewed despite Priority 3 spec saying 'prefer large text rewrites' first.
audit_data.throughput_observed: ~3-4 reviews/day, ~1-2 from change-detect. New decider work adds back ~1-2 candidates/day. Net steady-state, not draining the deep tail.

context.scope_of_prop: Backward-compat fix for fingerprint comparison when older schemas are missing newer fields. NOT a fingerprint-format migration (would force re-fingerprinting all 462 reviews). Operator's lean: handle missing-old-field as a forward-only signal — if a field exists in the current data but not in the old fingerprint, treat it as 'field was added since last review → flag for review'. Or alternatively, when old-fingerprint is missing fields, fall back to comparing only the fields both fingerprints have (so detail_verdict drift still triggers even if tldr_* is missing-old). Tinker argues which is more correct.
context.why_now: The deep-tail items aren't actively breaking anything (the c2 reviews may still be substantively correct, just stale on surface text), but the structural problem WILL re-emerge every time we add a new fingerprint field — any time the schema evolves, every review predating that schema becomes invisible to change-detect. This is a recurring failure mode, not a one-time April-7 issue.
context.why_not_just_force_re_review: Operator considered queueing the 14 deep-tail items via priority-queue as a one-time clear-out. Rejected in favor of structural fix because: (a) the bug class repeats every schema evolution, (b) a queue push is a one-time triage tax, the structural fix is a one-time engineering tax with no recurring cost, (c) the deep-tail items themselves may be fine — what matters is that future schema evolutions don't strand similar tails.

deliverable_requested.output_file: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-curmudgeon-fingerprint-compare-fix.json (assign next available PROP number after PROP-017)

structure.verification_pass: Confirm or refute the diagnosis. Trace the change-detection drift comparison through curmudgeon's prompt + reference material. Run a synthetic compare on WIN-050 (current fingerprint vs c2 fingerprint at monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-050.c2.json) to verify what curmudgeon's logic would output. Report: does it flag for review or silently skip? Show the trace.
structure.root_cause_classification: If diagnosis confirms: classify as schema-evolution-stranding (every fingerprint field added in the future leaves all prior reviews invisible to change-detect). If diagnosis refutes: identify the actual reason the deep tail isn't getting popped, and propose a fix for that.
structure.fix_options_compared: Lay out at least 3 fix options, with costs and risks: (1) Forward-only treatment: missing-old-field = 'field was added → flag for review' regardless of new value. (2) Reduced-set comparison: when old fingerprint is missing fields, compare only fields both have; if any common field shows >20% drift, flag. (3) Lazy backfill: on first encounter, re-derive old fingerprint from the review's text fields if recorded, else from the WIN's git-blamed state at review-time. Argue which is least likely to cause regression and most likely to handle future schema evolutions. Operator's lean is option 1 or 2; argue.
structure.implementation_sketch: For the chosen fix option, sketch the change to monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md (or wherever the comparison is specified). Use prose, not code — curmudgeon executes the comparison in the LLM, not via a script. The fix is a prompt edit that explicitly handles the missing-old-field case.
structure.deep_tail_disposition: Once the fix lands, what happens to the 14 deep-tail items? Will they auto-pop on the next change-detect run? If yes, in what order? Estimate the cycles needed to clear them at current 4h cadence. If the fix doesn't auto-pop them (e.g., because the prompt edit only affects future comparisons), specify whether a one-time priority-queue push of the 14 items is needed alongside the fix.
structure.side_effects: Will the fix introduce false positives elsewhere? E.g., if every old-schema review now flags as 'fields added → review needed', does change-detect get flooded with 199-some old reviews all flagging at once? How does the fix avoid creating its own version of the stale-loop?
structure.regression_test: How would we verify the fix worked? Specify: pre-fix, run change-detect synthetically against the deep-tail 14 + a control of recently-cycled items, confirm the 14 don't pop. Post-fix, re-run, confirm at least the highest-drift items now pop. Define disprove conditions.
structure.future_schema_evolution_protection: What changes to the curmudgeon prompt or fingerprint format would prevent the next schema evolution from stranding more reviews? Suggestions: explicit version field on fingerprint, schema migration policy, or a 'compare ONLY common fields' default. Argue.

deliverable_requested.do_not_implement: DO NOT modify monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md, or any review files. Do NOT push priority-queue entries for the deep tail. Operator reviews PROP and authorizes implementation in a separate step.
open_design_questions_for_tinker_to_addr: Should the fix preserve the existing prioritization order (verdict changes > large text rewrites > new items > TLDR-only changes), or does the missing-field case demand a new priority bucket?

If we choose option 1 (forward-only — missing-field = added → flag), how do we avoid all 199 old-schema reviews popping in a single run when the fix lands? Should the fix include a one-time soak (e.g., process at most 1 deep-tail item per run for the first N runs)?
Should the fix also write a log/note when it triggers on missing-field-added, so operator can audit the catch rate?
Curmudgeon's tracker.json — does it carry any state that would interact with this fix? Verify and call out.

non_goals: Do NOT propose a full fingerprint-format migration that requires re-fingerprinting all 462 existing reviews.
Do NOT propose changes to the priority-queue routing logic — that's a separate concern from change-detection eligibility.
Do NOT propose changes to digest-reviews.js, decider's processing of reviews, or any review consumers — focus is on curmudgeon's eligibility check.
Do NOT broaden scope to other curmudgeon design issues — focus narrowly on the missing-old-field stranding bug.

proposed_fix_spec.note_to_tinker: Operator's lean is option 1 or 2 (forward-only or reduced-set comparison). If you propose a fundamentally different approach, argue for it explicitly. The 14 deep-tail items aren't actively harming the review (their c2 content may still be substantively correct), so the fix can be unhurried — quality of the structural design matters more than speed of clearing the deep tail.

completion_criteria.file_present: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-curmudgeon-fingerprint-compare-fix.json exists with all 8 deliverable structure sections.
completion_criteria.diagnosis_verified: Either confirmed or refuted via empirical trace on a real deep-tail item (WIN-050 or similar).
completion_criteria.options_compared: At least 3 fix options laid out with costs/risks, recommendation argued.
completion_criteria.deep_tail_disposition_specified: Clear statement on whether the 14 items auto-pop after the fix, and if not, what additional action is needed.
completion_criteria.side_effects_addressed: Specifically: how does the fix avoid creating a 199-review-flood when it lands?
completion_criteria.future_protection_argued: Concrete proposal for preventing the next schema-evolution stranding event.
completion_criteria.open_questions_addressed: All 4 open design questions answered with reasoning, not deferred.
completion_criteria.mark_directive_completed: Set status:'completed' with completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored fields appended per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.

cross_references: DIRECTIVE-20260422-003-curmudgeon-design-rubric.json (parallel curmudgeon design improvement, in case findings interact)
